description: peer-send retry with a message-id that already has an inbox row must short-circuit via the dedup probe: no new insert, same server_seq returned, plain output carries the ' [deduped]' suffix. Pre-seed includes one inbox row with server_seq=7 and idempotency_key='01HXDEDUPDEDUPDEDUPDEDUPDEDU' plus a matching peer_rooms turn_count=7 so the state post-retry is identical to pre-state.
pre_sql: INSERT INTO sessions (cwd, label, agent, role, session_key, pair_key, started_at, last_seen_at) VALUES ('__CWD__', 'alice', 'claude', 'peer', 'alice-key', NULL, '2099-01-01T00:00:00Z', '2099-01-01T00:00:00Z'); INSERT INTO sessions (cwd, label, agent, role, session_key, pair_key, started_at, last_seen_at) VALUES ('__CWD__', 'bob', 'claude', 'peer', 'bob-key', NULL, '2099-01-01T00:00:00Z', '2099-01-01T00:00:00Z'); INSERT INTO inbox (to_cwd, to_label, from_cwd, from_label, body, created_at, room_key, workspace_id, idempotency_key, server_seq) VALUES ('__CWD__', 'bob', '__CWD__', 'alice', 'same message', '2099-01-01T00:00:00Z', 'cwd:__CWD__#alice+bob', 'default', '01HXDEDUPDEDUPDEDUPDEDUPDEDU', 7); INSERT INTO peer_rooms (room_key, pair_key, turn_count, terminated_at, terminated_by) VALUES ('cwd:__CWD__#alice+bob', NULL, 7, NULL, NULL);
